audit_status.source_base: Hebrew Numbers / Bemidbar Balaam spirit-oracles and nations-oracle text tradition, draft aligned later
audit_status.review_note: First-pass draft reviewed for Balaam abandoning enchantments, spirit reception, Mah Tovu tents oracle, Agag/king imagery, third blessing, star-and-scepter oracle, Edom/Amalek/Kenite/Asshur/Kittim nations sayings, and departure.

verses.restored: Balaam saw that it was good in eyes of YHWH to bless Israel, and he did not go as time after time toward enchantments, but set his face toward wilderness.
Balaam lifted his eyes and saw Israel dwelling by its tribes, and spirit of Divine came upon him.
He took up his oracle and said: Declaration of Balaam son of Beor, declaration of man whose eye is opened,
declaration of one hearing sayings of El, who sees vision of Shaddai, falling down yet with eyes uncovered.
How good are your tents, Jacob, your dwelling-places, Israel!
Like valleys stretched out, like gardens beside river, like aloes YHWH planted, like cedars beside waters.
Water flows from his buckets, and his seed is in many waters; his king shall be higher than Agag, and his kingdom shall be exalted.
El brings him out from Egypt; like horns/strength of wild ox is he for him. He shall consume nations, his adversaries, break their bones, and crush with his arrows.
He crouches, he lies down like lion and like lioness; who will raise him? Blessed are those blessing you, and cursed are those cursing you.
Anger of Balak burned against Balaam, and he struck his palms together. Balak said to Balaam, To curse my enemies I called you, and look, you have surely blessed these three times.
Now flee to your place. I said I would honor you greatly, but look, YHWH has held you back from honor.
Balaam said to Balak, Did I not also speak to your messengers whom you sent to me, saying,
If Balak gives me his house full of silver and gold, I cannot cross mouth/command of YHWH to do good or evil from my own heart; what YHWH speaks, that I shall speak?
Now look, I am going to my people. Come, I will counsel/tell you what this people will do to your people in latter days.
He took up his oracle and said: Declaration of Balaam son of Beor, declaration of man whose eye is opened,
declaration of one hearing sayings of El and knowing knowledge of Most High, who sees vision of Shaddai, falling down yet with eyes uncovered.
I see him, but not now; I behold him, but not near. A star steps forth from Jacob, and a scepter rises from Israel; it shall crush corners of Moab and break down all sons of tumult/Sheth.
Edom shall become a possession, Seir shall become possession of its enemies, and Israel shall do valiantly.
One from Jacob shall rule and destroy survivor from city.
He saw Amalek, took up his oracle, and said: First of nations was Amalek, but his end is destruction.
He saw Kenite, took up his oracle, and said: Enduring is your dwelling, and your nest is set in rock.
Yet Kain shall be consumed; how long until Asshur carries you captive?
He took up his oracle and said: Alas, who shall live when El appoints this?
Ships shall come from hand/coast of Kittim; they shall afflict Asshur and afflict Eber, and he also shall come to destruction.
Balaam rose and went and returned to his place, and Balak also went his way.

translation_notes: Balaam abandons his repeated search for enchantments and receives spirit while viewing Israel by tribes; a foreign seer becomes a vehicle of blessing.
'Whose eye is opened' is lexically uncertain and has received multiple interpretations; preserve the ambiguity for audit.
Numbers 24:5, Mah tovu, became a major Jewish liturgical verse. Its Jewish reception must be honored without Christian supersession or appropriation.
Agag may refer to a royal figure or title and requires historical/textual audit.
The star-and-scepter oracle has extensive Jewish, Christian, and political reception history. The restored layer does not collapse it into one later claimant.
Violent domination language toward Moab, Edom, Amalek, and other peoples belongs to ancient royal-war oracle rhetoric and cannot authorize modern ethnic, territorial, or religious violence.
The final nations sayings are compressed and textually difficult, especially Kain, Asshur, Eber, and Kittim.
safety_note: Do not use Numbers 24 to justify ethnic supremacy, conquest, antisemitism, supersessionism, messianic violence, or modern territorial claims. Preserve the beauty of blessing and Jewish liturgical continuity while treating the nations-oracle violence as ancient rhetoric under ethical restraint.
